options.label: Staff tier — Manager & Architect (fallback order — fable recommended)
options.help: Long-running, unsupervised, taste-heavy work: design + coordination. First entry is the working model; the rest are fallbacks, applied by falling UP to a smarter model when one is unavailable. fable is the recommended default. (haiku is intentionally excluded.)

options.label: Senior tier — Builder & structural preflight (fallback order — falls UP)
options.help: Well-defined execution against a locked spec, and structure/spec-conformance preflight review. Fall UP to a smarter model when one is unavailable, never down.

options.label: Junior tier — simple tasks & the codex-runner wrapper (fallback order)
options.help: Straightforward, low-floor Claude tasks, and the thin sonnet wrapper around the Codex CLI. Falls UP if unavailable.

options.label: Gate reviews on the external reviewer tier (gpt-5.5 / Codex)
Reviewer tier — cross-model correctness review (gpt-5.5, outside the hierarchy)
options.help: Reviews Anthropic-written code with a different model to avoid a same-model blind spot. Runs via `codex review`. Only used when the reviewer gate is on.

options.label: Mechanical tier — computer-use & bulk work (gpt-5.5 / Codex)
options.help: Effectively-free bulk mechanical work and computer-use (simulators, screenshots, app launching, runtime inspection) via the Codex CLI. Used by the codex-computer-use skill.

options.label: Skip the approval gate after the spec (auto-advance to build)
